'''[Notice]'''
{{{reason|This is a '''RESTRICTED''' document. The following document can only be viewed by high-ranking personnel (Level 5 Clearance). Any person who does not fall under this role will be tracked down and terminated. This document is property of the ███}}}
{{Class Deadzone}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Castus and Colossus.jpg|caption=An artistic description of the level's starting point.}} If you are reading this document, then this means you are qualified to know about this dangerous level. The following information is on a "need to know" basis, and any mention of any of the information present in this document should be presented only in controlled environments, such as ███ sanctioned meetings with the appropriate people. Only specific, high-ranking officials should have access to any of this information. If you suspect any unauthorized personnel have gained access to said information or could potentially gain unauthorized access, report said activity immediately to the appropriate, high-ranking officers. If you see something, say something. They will be swiftly dealt with. Level 271, nicknamed Castolossus's Lair, was previously only rumored in its existence. It was given its 271 designation due to it being suspected of residing outside of [[Level 2]] and leading to [[Level 71]]. There are no images depicting Level 271, but we were able to have an artist create a visual based on the description of the one, single survivor of this level. The level is depicted as a long bridge, expanding over a deep expanse. The lone survivor described the depths to be thousands of feet below the wooden bridge with twisting runs of pipes, like lobes of the brain, racing and turning into impossible shapes. The familiar pipes, alongside literature discovered in ruins as well as conversations with [[The King of Normality|Subject 0]], suggest that this "lair" resides above [[Level 2]]. Adjacent to the bridge is a large castle with crenelations and turret towers, as well as a single-draw bridge that opens when approached. Once inside, the lights will flicker momentarily before illuminating the stone walls and floors entirely. The anonymous wanderer described many pylon-like structures with gears and shafts twisting near the corners and ceiling. These mechanical structures were noted to create a rhythmic symphony of clanks and clicks. There were noted to be multiple loose stones in the ground that would activate traps via pressure, such as automatic crossbows that fire in the direction of the trespasser, or boiling tar being released from unknown sources from the ceiling. The surviving wanderer had received several injuries, but also managed a few lucky dodges when attempting to traverse the entrance of this castle. ==Notable Locations== While traversing Level 271, the wanderer described several notable locations throughout the castle. Though some sources indicate that there might be many more secret passages and corridors throughout the castle, as well as the possibility of a cult hidden somewhere in its depths, only a few locations were noted to exist as described physically. ===The Library=== The library is described as notably quiet, lacking any sort of mechanical gears or other noisy machines. Inside is a single wooden chair, ornate in design with a red velvet cushion on the seat and backrest. Next to the chair is a simple wooden table with a dimly lit oil lamp. The walls are entirely lined with bookshelves, with the only exception being the entrance. The wanderer stated that when looking at these books, hoping to find potential secrets of the level, they discovered that each book appeared to be a different version or translation of a famous religious text, such as The Bible or the Quran, upon further observation. Any time the wanderer opened one of the books, they described a lump forming in their throat that would squeeze their air passageway until it was impossible to breathe, as though hands were reaching out from the books and wrapping around their neck, squeezing relentlessly. ===The Dining Room=== In the middle of this large castle is a grand dining table, illuminated by a chandelier of extraordinary grandeur. Situated on the table are platters covered in cloches, resting on a red and gold-patterned tablecloth. The seats are of the same design as the single chair from the library, wood with red velvet cushions. The walls, however, presented a great hazard, as they were covered systematically from floor to ceiling in sharp, bladed weapons, pointing inwards towards the tables. The anonymous wanderer, still keen on the potential for traps, delicately removed one of the cloches from a platter. Underneath was a magnificent bird, with a hot, crispy, seasoned skin with garnishes and stuffings inside. Once the cloche was removed, however, the wanderer noticed a change in the clicks and clanks of the ever-present mechanical percussive battery and moved to the side. Moments later, blades from the wall launched towards the center of the room. A lethal trap that they had somehow avoided. ===The Room of Artifacts=== This room is a large, rectangular stone room with red, triangular-tipped banners lining the walls. Along the edges of the room, extraordinarily tall pillars are situated, each displaying a special artifact as if they were trophies of some sort. Simple wooden ladders leaned on each of the pillars, allowing for closer observation of the treasures resting on the pillar tops. Handling these artifacts is not advisable, as the anonymous wanderer noted that attempting to touch these items caused quick, intense burns to their hands. Next to the artifacts were golden plaques with each of their individual names carved into them. The artifacts were described as follows:
|-| A Sign From The Universe= '''A Sign From The Universe''' is an artifact that appears to be a mysterious glass object, shaped into a strange, symmetrical, and bulbous symbol. Inside the glass is an unsettling liquid. The wanderer, at first sight, described the liquid to be "space" itself, and that upon closer inspection of the liquid, planets, stars, and even galaxies seem to swirl systematically within. There have been many theories regarding this liquid, with the most popular one stating that this artifact came from somewhere beyond the universe and that '''Castolossus''', the level's native entity, found it and brought it back to the Backrooms. Another theory suggests that this liquid is a depiction of the universe before it was swallowed by [[The Void]], and that it was the key to restoring the universe to what it once was. |-| A Fragment Of Time= '''A Fragment Of Time''' resembles a fragment described as liquid, solid, and gas at the same time. The wanderer described having visions of their family members and reliving memories when staring into the artifact, which psychologically tormented them to the point of mental injury, giving them symptoms of psychosis. Some suggest that this fragment contains every universe in one, letting one see all possible timelines for all possible scenarios. There is a lack of evidence supporting this theory. |-| The One True Mind= '''The One True Mind''' is a horrifying and disturbing artifact. It resembles a sentient brain, chopped up into many pieces. It has tentacle-like appendages, and it can move around by itself in its own space. The artifact is locked in a glass chamber, presumably because if not, the artifact would use its tentacles to flee the castle, unleashing an unfathomable horror across the Backrooms. Rumors state that '''Castolossus''' cut the brain of every deceased wanderer in the Backrooms and gave it sentience. Due to the erratic and violent movement of this seemingly living entity, it is presumed to be full of anger and malice. |-| A Million Souls= '''A Million Souls''' is an artifact that, when looked at, can blind a person due to its brightness. Its appearance is that of lots of floating, colorful orbs. Literature found on the object suggests that '''Castolossus''' captures the souls of every deceased wanderer and locks them in an indestructible chamber. The souls' color ranges from bright yellow to dark red, with some people saying that the color depends on the wanderer's personality and the decisions that the wanderer has made throughout their life.
===The Throne Room=== The Throne Room seems to be the very center of the castle, and of Level 271. All mechanized cogs, shafts, and wheels all seem to operate from the forces generated in this colossal space. Towards the back of this massive chamber is a large golden throne where the level's native entity, '''Castolossus''' sits. The metallic structures that make up the entity's body, as well as the grand throne and all the structures present in the grand hall, are illuminated by large, ornate chandeliers and enormous windows that line the walls near the top. The floor, unlike the typical stone found throughout the castle, is made of black and white marble tiles. The marbling is that of a golden trim that glistens with the ambient light of the grand throne room. Upon entering, the wanderer described an army of the undead, climbing through the marble tiles, as though they were climbing from underneath, despite the tiles appearing entirely solid. These animated corpses will surround the wanderer and pile themselves on top of their victims, attempting to suffocate them to death. Their grips and grapples are notably strong, despite their slow movement. If one manages to escape their grasp, they must confront '''Castolossus'''. There has only been one recorded survivor. ==Entities== There is only one entity present in '''Level 271''', known as: ===Castolossus=== '''Castolossus''' is a god-like being, presumably the master of time, as it has the power to stop, change, and alter time itself, though only for short durations, which gives '''Castolossus''' a ginormous advantage during a duel. It spends most of its time sitting on its throne, according to the wanderer, but it apparently will sometimes get up to meet with other notable beings such as [[King of Normality|Subject 0]], etc. The biology of '''Castolossus''' is described as having two metallic heads without mouths and glowing purple eyes. The entity has two arms with abnormally elongated fingers that operate like snakes. Coiled within one of these finger-like appendages is a long staff with a green, glowing jewel at the top. The rumored cultists call this staff the "Time Staff", and it is believed to be the source of '''Castolossus's''' time-altering power. All who have confronted '''Castolossus''' have perished in combat, except for one single wanderer. This wanderer was presumably tasked by '''Castolossus''' to spread this message: "Leave me be or perish." This message was presumably intended for organizations such as the M.E.G. or other wanderers in the Backrooms. Due to conversations with other entities as well as important background information discovered in ancient ruins, it is presumed that this entity is well within its capabilities to eliminate all life as we know it, possibly colliding timelines together in such a violent fashion that all life might suddenly cease to exist. As high-ranking members of ███, we implore that all avoid this level entirely to avoid the wrath of this malevolent being. Thus, mentions of the existence of this level are highly prohibited in avoidance of the threat of total annihilation. It is unknown whether '''Castolossus''' can use its powers outside the range of the level. ==Colonies and Outposts== According to the wanderer's testimony, there is an alleged "cult" that resides in '''Level 271''': ===The Cult Of 271=== * Has a distorted anthem *Consists of around 30 members *Will attack anyone who disrespects or mocks their culture
"''Tr ust n o on e.''.'''''"'''''
- ???
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *It is rumored that if you encounter '''Castolossus''' in another level without getting caught, you will be sent here. *It is rumored that if you tear through the ceiling of [[Level 2]], you may end up here. ===Exits=== *It is rumored that if you "die" by '''Castolossus''', there is a chance you will be sent to a random Class 5 Level. *The sole survivor of '''Castolossus''' was transported to [[Level 71]] after being confronted, but it is rumored that one could be sent to any level. {{Collapse|initial='''Do you want to gain more information about this level?'''|final='''Do you want to gain more information about this level?'''|body= So you're the person who wanted to learn more about my lair?
Well, you will not be getting any valuable information out of me.
I would much rather you mind your own business.
However, before you go, I suppose I shall teach you a meaningful lesson.
The march of time cannot be stopped. Only delayed.|num=151}}|username=Additional User Verification Required|password=Enter your PIN and Token ID}}}}}}}} {{Collapse|initial=Open Author Credits?|final=Close Author Credits?|body= Rewriter(s): [[User:TheDadams|Dadams]], [[User:NormalDudeWithaNormalLife|Normal]]
